scheme_name: Replanting & Rejuvenation of Coconut Garden" by the Coconut Development Board
page_title: Replanting & Rejuvenation of Coconut Garden" by the Coconut Development Board

details: The scheme “Replanting & Rejuvenation of Coconut Garden (R&R)" by the Coconut Development Board was launched by the Department of Agriculture and Farmer's Welfare, Union Territory of Puducherry. Through this scheme, the coconut farmers are provided financial assistance through DBT (Direct Benefit Transfer) mode for the cutting & removal, replanting, and for integrated management practices.
benefits: @ ₹ 1,000/‐ per palm subject to a maximum of ₹ 32,000/ per hectare.
@ ₹ 40 per seedling subject to a maximum of ₹ 4,000.
@ ₹ 17,500/‐ per hectare in two annual installments, for upto 4 hectares per farmer.
eligibility: All categories of coconut farmers with farmer's identity cards/ authenticated land records/documents are eligible.

offline: The interested applicant should visit (during office hours) the
Uzhavar Udhaviyagam (Farmers' Help Centres) or the Office of the Additional Director of Agriculture (Horticulture)
and request a hard copy of the
of the application form from the Agricultural Officer.

In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste the passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
Submit the duly filled and signed application form and the documents to the concerned authority.

On receipt of the application, necessary entries will be made in the application receipt register and a unique number is assigned to the applicant for identifying each applicant which shall be used for all further references and also as acknowledgement for the receipt of the application.

documents_required: In the case of land owners cultivating their land, a copy of the farmer identity card containing the details of survey numbers and the extent of land holding needs to be submitted along with the application form.
If no identity card is issued to the farmer to date a copy of the land document (sale deed/ partition deed/ gift deed or will) needs to be submitted.

The tenants cultivating lands owned by religious Institutions/Wakfs/Mutts/Trusts/ Municipalities shall produce a letter of consent from the authority of the institutions concerned as prescribed by the Department.
Further, tenants shall produce a cultivation certificate from the land owner or Agriculture Department / Revenue Department who carried out joint verification for the purpose.

faqs.question: How is the financial assistance for cutting & removal calculated under the R&R scheme?
faqs.answer: Coconut farmers receive ₹1,000 per palm, with a maximum limit of ₹32,000 per hectare for cutting & removal.

faqs.question: Can tenants cultivating lands owned by Municipalities apply for the R&R scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, tenants need consent from the municipality and a cultivation certificate for application.

faqs.question: What is the time frame for the seasonality of benefits under the R&R scheme?
faqs.answer: The benefits, including cutting & removal, replanting, and integrated management practices, are provided annually.

faqs.question: Are there any restrictions on the maximum area for availing Integrated Management Practices support?
faqs.answer: Yes, farmers can receive support for integrated management practices for up to 4 hectares under the R&R scheme.

faqs.question: How should tenants cultivating lands owned by religious institutions obtain consent for the R&R scheme?
faqs.answer: Tenants need a letter of consent from the concerned authority and a cultivation certificate from the landowner or relevant departments.

faqs.question: What documentation is needed if a farmer does not possess an identity card?
faqs.answer: In the absence of an identity card, farmers should submit land documents like sale deed, partition deed, gift deed, or will.

faqs.question: Who is eligible to benefit from the "Replanting & Rejuvenation of Coconut Garden (R&R)" scheme?
faqs.answer: All categories of coconut farmers with a farmer's identity card or authenticated land records are eligible.

faqs.question: How frequently are the benefits distributed under the R&R scheme?
faqs.answer: The benefits, including cutting & removal, replanting, and integrated management practices, are provided annually.

faqs.question: What is the financial assistance for Integrated Management Practices under the R&R scheme?
faqs.answer: Coconut farmers can avail ₹17,500 per hectare in two annual installments, for up to 4 hectares per farmer.

faqs.question: What is the assistance provided for replanting in the R&R scheme?
faqs.answer: Farmers receive ₹40 per seedling, with a maximum limit of ₹4,000 for replanting support.

faqs.question: What is the financial support offered for cutting & removal under the R&R scheme?
faqs.answer: Coconut farmers receive ₹1,000 per palm, with a maximum limit of ₹32,000 per hectare for cutting & removal.
